economic_notes: First Nation receives gaming revenue from Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (1.7% of aggregate provincial gross gaming revenue, monthly payments). Funds restricted to community development, health, education, cultural and economic development. Sheila Childforever Memorial Economic Development Corporation is a consolidated subsidiary. Major capital projects underway: Water Treatment Plant, Hydro Compound, Rapid Housing Initiatives, Bridge Project, Water & Wastewater Project, Backup Power Project.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to Asset Retirement Obligations (PS 3280) — auditors could not confirm completeness of ARO liabilities. First Nation consolidates Sheila Childforever Memorial Economic Development Corporation. Large assets under construction ($12.4M of $15.5M TCA) including Water Treatment Plant, Hydro Compound, and Rapid Housing Initiatives. ISC revenue per note 13 is $3,124,299 but statement shows $3,142,299 — note 13 figure used for isc_funding. Non-compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act posting deadline noted. Prior year had $500,000 Drinking Water Settlement (one-time) but not in current year. No long-term debt. No schedule of remuneration included in available text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($3,124,299) + Ministry of Northern Development and Mines ($1,696,748) + Ministry of Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation ($85,000) + SLAAMS ($105,238) = $5,011,285; OSR = Other revenue ($534,710) + OFNLP ($1,090,345) minus deferred adjustments not separately isolatable — OSR approximated as total revenue less identifiable government transfers.
